title: Event journal
analysis:
The task requires implementing an append-only EventJournal in reify-eval that records evaluation events (Started, Completed, Cancelled, Failed, CacheHit, WarmStartUsed) dual-indexed by time (BTreeMap<Instant>) and NodeId (HashMap<NodeId>). The journal will be owned by Engine and populated during eval() and edit_param() calls.

The existing codebase has:
- Engine struct in lib.rs (972 lines) with eval(), edit_param(), eval_cached(), check(), build() methods
- NodeId enum in cache.rs (Value, Constraint, Realization, Resolution variants) — derives Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash
- EvalOutcome enum in cache.rs (Changed, Unchanged) — derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq
- VersionId in reify-types (u64 newtype, implements Ord, Copy, Clone, Hash)
- CacheStore.record_evaluation() pattern showing natural instrumentation points
- Test infrastructure: reify-test-support with bracket_compiled_module(), MockConstraintChecker, CompiledModuleBuilder, TopologyTemplateBuilder

Key code points where events should be recorded:
1. eval() lines 194-413: Pass 1 (Param/Auto cells), Pass 2 (Let bindings), Resolution phase — each cache.record_evaluation() call is a natural Started→Completed pair
2. edit_param() lines 424-542: eval set loop with early cutoff — Started before eval, Completed{outcome} after, skipped nodes get no events
3. eval_cached() lines 550-738: cache hits via try_fast_path or not-dirty checks → CacheHit events; cache misses → Started/Completed

The module structure follows existing patterns: journal.rs alongside cache.rs, deps.rs, etc. with `pub mod journal;` in lib.rs. Unit tests go in #[cfg(test)] module within journal.rs; integration tests in tests/journal.rs.

Instant is suitable for timestamps since it's monotonic and implements Ord (BTreeMap key requirement). The journal is in-process only — serialization not needed for v0.1.

prerequisites.description: Create empty crates/reify-eval/src/journal.rs module file and add `pub mod journal;` to lib.rs module declarations. Add `pub use journal::*;` re-export to lib.rs.

steps.description: Write unit tests in journal.rs #[cfg(test)] for EvalEvent, EventKind, and EventPayload type construction: verify all EventKind variants can be created (Started, Completed{outcome: Changed}, Completed{outcome: Unchanged}, Cancelled, Failed{error}, CacheHit, WarmStartUsed), all EventPayload variants (Duration, Error, Custom), EvalEvent can be constructed with all fields, and Clone/Debug derives work.

Define EvalEvent struct {timestamp: Instant, node_id: NodeId, kind: EventKind, version: VersionId, payload: Option<EventPayload>}, EventKind enum {Started, Completed{outcome: EvalOutcome}, Cancelled, Failed{error: String}, CacheHit, WarmStartUsed}, EventPayload enum {Duration(std::time::Duration), Error(String), Custom(String)}. All derive Clone, Debug. Import NodeId from crate::cache, EvalOutcome from crate::cache, VersionId from reify_types.

Write unit tests for EventJournal::new() and record(): new() creates empty journal (len()==0, is_empty()==true); record() appends event and increments len(); multiple record() calls maintain insertion order; events can be accessed by index via all_events() slice accessor.

Define EventJournal struct {events: Vec<EvalEvent>, by_time: BTreeMap<Instant, Vec<usize>>, by_node: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<usize>>}. Implement new() returning empty journal, record(&mut self, event: EvalEvent) that appends to events vec and updates both by_time and by_node indices with the new index, len() -> usize, is_empty() -> bool, all_events() -> &[EvalEvent].

Write unit tests for events_for_node(node_id): record events for 3 different NodeIds, verify events_for_node returns correct events for each, verify empty Vec for unknown NodeId, verify events are in insertion order within a node.
Implement events_for_node(&self, node_id: &NodeId) -> Vec<&EvalEvent> — look up indices in by_node HashMap, map to &self.events[idx] references. Return empty Vec if node not found.

Write unit tests for events_in_range(range): record events with naturally ordered Instants (Instant::now() between records), verify range query returns correct subset. Test with Range (start..end), RangeFrom (start..), and RangeFull (..). Test empty range returns empty Vec.

Implement events_in_range(&self, range: impl RangeBounds<Instant>) -> Vec<&EvalEvent> — use BTreeMap::range() on by_time to get matching time entries, collect all event indices, sort by index (preserves insertion order), deduplicate, and map to references.

Write unit tests for events_since(version): record events across VersionId(0), VersionId(1), VersionId(2), verify events_since(VersionId(1)) returns only events with version >= 1. Test exact boundary match and no-match case (version higher than all events returns empty).
Implement events_since(&self, version: VersionId) -> Vec<&EvalEvent> — linear scan of events vec filtering where event.version >= version. Returns references in insertion order.

Write unit tests for latest_for_node(node_id): record multiple events for same node, verify latest_for_node returns the last one recorded. Verify None for unknown node. Verify correct result after interleaving events for different nodes.
Implement latest_for_node(&self, node_id: &NodeId) -> Option<&EvalEvent> — look up by_node, get last index, return &self.events[last_idx].

Write integration test (tests/journal.rs) that Engine::eval() populates the journal: create Engine with MockConstraintChecker, eval bracket_compiled_module, verify engine.journal().len() > 0, verify events exist for known ValueCellIds (e.g., Bracket/width), verify all events have the correct VersionId matching the snapshot, verify Started events precede Completed events for same node.

Add journal: EventJournal field to Engine struct, initialize in new(). Add pub fn journal(&self) -> &EventJournal accessor. Wire into eval(): before each node evaluation call Instant::now() and record Started event, after cache.record_evaluation() record Completed{outcome} event with Duration payload. Cover all three passes: Param/Auto (pass 1), Let bindings (pass 2), and Resolution phase.

Write integration test that Engine::edit_param() records correct journal events: eval() then edit_param(), verify new events appear with the edit_param's version, verify Started/Completed pairs for re-evaluated Value nodes, verify events_since(edit_version) returns only the edit_param events, verify early-cutoff-skipped nodes do NOT have Started events.

Wire journal into edit_param(): record Started before reify_expr::eval_expr, record Completed{outcome} after cache.record_evaluation(). For Constraint/Realization nodes in eval set that are tracked but not evaluated, do not record events. Ensure version in events matches the edit_param's allocated VersionId(version_id).

Write integration test for eval_cached() journal events: first call (cold start) records Started/Completed for all nodes, second call with same version records CacheHit events via fast-path, call after set_param_and_invalidate records mix of CacheHit (unchanged nodes) and Started/Completed (dirty nodes).

Wire journal into eval_cached(): record CacheHit when try_fast_path succeeds, record CacheHit when cache reuse succeeds (not dirty, existing entry), record Started/Completed for cache misses that trigger actual evaluation. Include Duration payload on Completed events.

design_decisions.decision: Place journal.rs in crates/reify-eval/src/ alongside Engine code
design_decisions.rationale: The PRD mentions reify-runtime but Engine lives in reify-eval. Keeping the journal co-located with Engine avoids cross-crate coupling and allows direct field access. The journal can be re-exported from reify-runtime later if needed.

Instant is monotonic and implements Ord (required for BTreeMap keys). It's not serializable, but the EventJournal is an in-process data structure for live consumers (LSP, CLI). Serialization can be added later with a wrapper if needed. The task description explicitly specifies Instant.

design_decisions.decision: Return Vec<&EvalEvent> from query methods instead of &[EvalEvent]
design_decisions.rationale: Events are stored contiguously in a Vec but accessed via indices in secondary maps. Returning a contiguous slice is impossible when elements are scattered across non-contiguous positions. Vec<&EvalEvent> is the natural return type for index-based lookups.

design_decisions.decision: Use linear scan for events_since(version) instead of a version index
design_decisions.rationale: Version-based queries are expected to be infrequent (LSP polling, debugging). A linear scan over the events vec is simple and cache-friendly. A BTreeMap<VersionId, Vec<usize>> index can be added later if profiling shows it's needed.

design_decisions.decision: EventJournal is owned by Engine (not Arc<Mutex>)
design_decisions.rationale: Engine is already &mut self for eval/edit_param. The journal follows the same ownership model. M4 (concurrent evaluation) will need to revisit this with channels or Arc<Mutex>, but for now single-threaded ownership is correct and simple.

design_decisions.decision: Record events inline during eval/edit_param rather than via a callback/observer pattern
design_decisions.rationale: Direct recording is simpler and has zero allocation overhead beyond the event itself. An observer/subscriber pattern can be layered on top of the journal's query API by consumers. This avoids trait object dispatch overhead in the hot evaluation path.

design_decisions.decision: Do not record events for Constraint/Realization nodes tracked but not evaluated in edit_param
design_decisions.rationale: The existing code explicitly notes that Constraint/Realization nodes are 'tracked in eval set but not evaluated (deferred to check()/build())'. Recording Started without Completed would be misleading. Events for these node types will be added when check()/build() are instrumented.

design_decisions.decision: Include EvalOutcome in Completed variant rather than separate Changed/Unchanged event kinds
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the task description's specified EventKind::Completed{outcome: EvalOutcome}. Reuses the existing EvalOutcome enum from cache.rs. Keeps the event kind count manageable while encoding the early-cutoff-relevant information.

reuse.how: Used as the node_id field in EvalEvent and as HashMap key in by_node index. Already derives Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash — all traits needed for both roles.
Used inside EventKind::Completed{outcome: EvalOutcome} to record whether a node's result changed or was unchanged (enables early cutoff tracking). Already derives Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq.
Used as the version field in EvalEvent and as parameter to events_since(). Already derives Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, PartialOrd, Ord — Ord needed for version comparison in events_since().
record_evaluation() already returns EvalOutcome, which feeds directly into EventKind::Completed{outcome}. No extra computation needed to determine Changed vs Unchanged.
Reuse in integration tests to create a realistic CompiledModule with 5 params + 1 let binding for Engine eval/edit_param journal tests.
Reuse as the constraint_checker parameter when constructing Engine in integration tests.
For building custom minimal modules in targeted tests (e.g., single-node modules for event ordering verification).
Follow the same pattern for journal field: private field, pub fn journal(&self) -> &EventJournal accessor for testing/inspection.
